Xi Jinping:
Xi Jinping (born June 15, 1953) is a Chinese political leader and institution who serves as the General Secretary of the Chinese Communist Party (CPC) and Chairman of the State Military Commission, where he has served since 2012. He serves as the President of China and Chairman of the State Military Commission, where he has served since 2013. He is known as China’s fifth generation leader.
Xi Jinping was born in Beijing in the second marriage of Xi Zhongxun, a military member of a Chinese league’s military revolution, and during his father Xi Zhongxun’s reverse period in the Cultural Revolution he was exiled to a village called Liangjiahe in a specific area. He lived in a cave in Liangjiahe and after failing several times became a member of the Chinese Communist Party. He worked as a local party secretary. After three jobs he studied chemical engineering at Tsinghua University and increased political power in China’s coastal provinces.
From 1999 to 2002 he served as the governor of Fujian province and from 2002 to 2007 he served as the governor and party secretary of Zhejiang province. After that he appeared in China’s highest positions of power.
